agenda_pressure: Favor exact subsystem equivalences and rule-theoretic characterizations over nearby corollaries.
Spend asymmetry pressure on left interfaces and search bridges because right translation is already complete enough.
Reward results that connect fragment predicates, proof objects, and search procedures in one package.
Use negative results mainly when they isolate mixed-orientation escape or derivation-level non-rigidity.
current_bottlenecks: LeftRep still lacks the clean intrinsic subsystem presentation already available on the right.
One-step atomic reduction laws have not yet been lifted to reusable recursive normal forms.
Candidate syntax and prove1-style restricted search are not yet exact tests for one-sided membership.
Focused witness iff theorems exist, but witness-level substitution closure is still missing.
desired_summary_changes: An exact pure-left or left least-closure presentation that puts LeftRep on the same intrinsic footing as RightRep.
Bilateral recursive head-normal-form theorems for atomic conclusions, not only one-step reductions.
Exact candidate or restricted-search equivalences for one-sided fragment membership.
Focused witness theories that are compositional under same-orientation substitution rather than merely existential.
important_verified_counterexamples: For every derivation d : Γ ⇒ A, if every formula in A :: Γ is LeftDivisionOnly then d can be transformed into a derivation of Γ ⇒ A using no right-division rules, and if every formula in A :: Γ is RightDivisionOnly then d can be transfor...
∀ N : Nat, 2 ≤ N → ∃ Γ : List Tp, ∃ A : Tp, list_degree Γ + tp_degree A = N ∧ Γ ⇒ A ∧ FocusedTree (N + 1) Γ A ∧ ∀ m < N + 1, ¬ FocusedTree m Γ A
∀ N : Nat, ∃ Γ : List Tp, ∃ A : Tp, list_degree Γ + tp_degree A = N ∧ Γ ⇒ A ∧ FocusedTree (N + 1) Γ A ∧ ∀ m < N + 1, ¬ FocusedTree m Γ A
missing_bridges: From LeftRep to an exact pure-left or rule-theoretic intrinsic presentation.
From one-step atomic reductions to full recursive left and right head-normal forms.
From candidates and restricted search to exact LeftRep and RightRep criteria.
From existential homogeneous witnesses to compositional cut-stable focused proof objects.

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize pure-left or rule-theoretic intrinsic characterizations together with exact candidate or restricted-search bridges that make one-sided membership algorithmically and structurally internal.
next_direction.label: left_internalization_and_search_bridges
next_direction.rationale: Right-side internalization is already in hand, while the left subsystem interface and search-level exactness remain the clearest missing bridges; atomic-context escape and failed singleton and depth programs show that overlap and quantitative variants are lower leverage.

overexplored_patterns: Overlap-to-singleton and bidirectional-rigidity corollaries near the current atomic-overlap theorem.
One-way forgetting lemmas from homogeneous witnesses to ambient focusing or derivability.
Singleton and atomic-context variants that do not build subsystem or search interfaces.
Pure depth, degree, or candidate-count sharpness questions detached from fragment internalization.

anti_goals: Avoid cosmetic reformulations that merely rename connectives, restate existing proofs in different notation, or package known systems without introducing new structural understanding.
Avoid ad hoc proliferation of structural rules introduced only to fit isolated computational or linguistic effects, without algebraic necessity, symmetry, or metatheoretic justification.
Avoid one-off examples, special parameters, and local variants whose only purpose is to capture a narrow phenomenon and which do not contribute to a reusable classification.
Avoid collections of negative instance results that do not culminate in a separation theorem, obstruction principle, or exact boundary statement.
canonical_targets: Determine the structural classification of Lambek extensions generated by controlled additions of exchange, weakening, contraction, and related directional principles.
Identify the exact boundary at which cut elimination, decidability, and complexity fail or change across disciplined families of Lambek extensions.
Formulate a sharp completeness and incompleteness theory for $L$ and its major extensions over semantically natural model classes.
Discover a structural account of the interface between Lambek derivations and categorial semantics that preserves the essential asymmetries of the calculus.
soft_constraints: Prefer standard Lambek notation and conservative extensions of it, so that new results remain legible as contributions to the established theory rather than as notation-driven rebrandings.
Prefer exact classifications, equivalence theorems, separation results, and boundary theorems over accumulations of examples, constructions, or case-specific derivations.
Prefer abstractions that remain valuable under future changes of method, and avoid dependence on transient computational fashions or tool-specific implementations.
Prefer structural principles that are algebraically motivated, proof-theoretically disciplined, and semantically interpretable, rather than tuned to produce isolated outcomes.
themes: Clarify the structural taxonomy of Lambek systems by identifying which extensions represent genuinely distinct proof-theoretic regimes and which amount to reducible variants.
Determine exact correspondences among structural rules, semantic model classes, and formal-language classes, so that proof theory, algebra, and generative capacity constrain one another sharply.
Identify the metatheoretic boundaries at which cut elimination, decidability, completeness, and complexity change, especially under partial or directional control of structural principles.
Formulate the syntax-semantics interface of categorial grammar in structural terms, so that semantic interpretation preserves proof-theoretic distinctions rather than collapsing them.
valued_problem_types: Clarify derivability, admissibility, reducibility, and independence relations among structural rules and rule schemata for $L$ and its principled extensions.
Determine when structural or logical rules are invertible, admissible, or normalization-preserving, and isolate the exact role these properties play in metatheoretic control.
Identify separation theorems that rigorously distinguish extensions by formulas, derivability classes, model classes, or language classes, rather than by isolated examples.
Formulate exact boundary results for cut elimination, decidability, and complexity under restricted exchange, weakening, contraction, and directional or partial variants thereof.

theory_snapshot: The theory now has exact derivation-level and focused-witness characterizations of both one-sided fragments, same-orientation cut closure, bilateral one-step atomic reduction laws, and an exact pure-right translation theorem, so the right fragment already behaves as an intrinsic subsystem. The main frontier is to match that strength on the left and to turn the atomic reduction and candidate machinery into exact recursive and proof-search interfaces rather than external recognizers. Verified boundary evidence constrains the agenda: atomic contexts can still derive sequents outside both one-sided fragments, singleton-rigidity programs have already failed outside the atomic singleton boundary, and focused-depth sharpness claims have failed, so overlap-collapse and quantitative sharpness variants are unlikely to change the summary.
